behaviors.description: Only files changed in the pull request are analyzed — unchanged files are not scanned.
behaviors.justification: This behavior requires distinguishing changed vs. unchanged files. A Unit test with mocked file lists might hide bugs in how the code discovers which files changed (e.g., incorrect git diff command or GitHub API parsing). Integration testing with a real git repository or mocked GitHub API response ensures the file filtering works correctly with realistic file sets.
behaviors.test_description: Set up a git repository with committed files and newly changed files. Mock the GitHub API to return a specific changed-file list (or use git diff). Run the linter and verify that findings only appear for files in the changed-file list, never for unchanged files.

behaviors.description: Findings are grouped by severity (error, warning, info) in the posted PR comment.
behaviors.justification: Grouping logic can be tested at Unit level, but the real failure boundary is whether the comment is actually posted to GitHub with correct structure. A mocked GitHub API might hide bugs in the actual API call (wrong endpoint, malformed payload, auth failures). Posting to a real GitHub API or test environment is necessary to catch real failures.
behaviors.test_description: Generate a set of mock findings with mixed severity levels. Post a PR comment to a test GitHub repository (or use a realistic GitHub API mock that validates payload format and HTTP behavior). Verify the posted comment text contains properly grouped sections for errors, warnings, and info findings.

behaviors.description: A pull request that touches 200 files completes analysis within the 10-minute Actions timeout.
behaviors.justification: Performance behavior cannot be tested with Unit mocks — they hide real execution costs. Integration testing with a realistic corpus of 200 files and real linter execution allows measurement of actual end-to-end time. System level (running in actual GitHub Actions) is more realistic but Integration is sufficient to validate timeout risk.
behaviors.test_description: Create a test corpus of 200 files with realistic linting complexity (varied file types, actual lint rule processing). Run the full analysis pipeline and measure total execution time. Assert time is under 10 minutes; capture per-phase timing (file discovery, linter execution, comment posting) to identify bottlenecks.

behaviors.description: When the linter process crashes on a malformed file, the remaining files are still analyzed and results posted.
behaviors.justification: This is error-handling for real subprocess crashes. Unit tests with mocked linter execution would hide the actual crash behavior and exception handling. Integration level (real linter subprocess, real malformed input) is needed to verify the crash is caught, logged, and subsequent files continue processing correctly.
behaviors.test_description: Create a test corpus with one deliberately malformed file (invalid syntax that causes linter crash) followed by valid files. Run the full pipeline and verify: (1) malformed file causes a crash or error, (2) error is caught and logged, (3) analysis continues for remaining valid files, (4) final comment includes findings from valid files and an error note for the malformed file.

behaviors.description: Duplicate findings on the same line from overlapping rules are deduplicated in the output.
behaviors.justification: Deduplication is pure logic with no I/O or external dependencies. Both input (finding objects) and output (deduplicated list) are in-memory data structures. A Unit test with sample findings fully validates the deduplication logic without needing integration with file discovery, linting, or GitHub APIs.
behaviors.test_description: Create a set of finding objects with identical line numbers, file paths, and descriptions but originating from different lint rules. Pass through the deduplication function and verify the output contains exactly one instance of each unique finding (deduplicated by line + description), regardless of rule origin.
